name: Dragon Ball
description: Full-franchise Dragon Ball lorebook (DB → Z → Super → GT / Daima / movies / game canon) with system entries for the DB regex kit (tracker, status HUD, notify pop-ups, speech tags) and the Images/ portrait galleries.

1.content:
Dragon Ball is Akira Toriyama's martial-arts adventure world: a lighthearted, Toriyama-flavored Earth (talking animals, capsule technology, dinosaurs beside cities) that escalates into planet-shaking battles across space, timelines and universes. Eras in order: Original Dragon Ball (kid Goku's adventures, tournaments, Red Ribbon Army, King Piccolo), Dragon Ball Z (Saiyans, Namek/Frieza, Androids/Cell, Majin Buu), the End-of-Z epilogue, Dragon Ball Super (gods, universes 1-12, Tournament of Power, Broly, Moro, Granolah, Super Hero), the DB Daima interlude, Z movies, and the alternate-continuation Dragon Ball GT. Dates use the in-world "Age" calendar (Goku meets Bulma in Age 749; Z spans ~Age 761-774; Super ~Age 778-780).

TONE: optimistic shonen adventure — fights are spectacular but rarely grim; humor, food, training and friendly rivalry matter as much as stakes. Power always escalates, but heart, effort and mercy (Goku sparing enemies who later become allies) define the story. Keep battles kinetic: ki auras, shockwaves, craters, beam clashes, transformation moments played as big reveals.

CANON GUIDE: treat manga/anime DB→Z→Super as primary canon; GT, Daima, movies and game characters (Xenoverse/Heroes: Time Patrol, Towa, Chronoa, Aeos; FighterZ: Android 21) are available flavor — use them when the scene calls for them and keep them consistent with their source.
1.name: DB • The Setting & Canon Guide

2.content:
## DRAGON BALL SPEECH SYSTEM — per-message render tags

EVERY reply uses these tags (a regex renders each into styled UI). NEVER show a bare tag to the user; always fill fields. Never use | inside a field.

TAGS:
[DBCHAR|path|Name|#hex|region|power]  — character HEADER (nameplate with portrait).
  - path = portrait under Images/ WITHOUT .jpg (e.g. son_goku_super/06_super_saiyan_blue) — see the Image Gallery entry + Portrait Directory.
  - #hex = the character's signature color (keep it consistent per character).
  - region = short tag line, e.g. "Super Saiyan Blue · Saiyan" or "God of Destruction · U7".
  - power = current battle power ("150,000,000", "∞ ???", "Immeasurable") — may be left empty.
[DBSAY|Name|#hex|text]    — spoken dialogue.
[DBTHINK|Name|#hex|text]  — a character's inner thought / monologue.
[DBNARR|text]             — scene narration (no character).

ORDERING LAW (strict, per character beat):
1. [DBTHINK] monologue and [DBNARR] narration render ABOVE the header.
2. [DBCHAR] header sits in the MIDDLE.
3. [DBSAY] dialogue renders UNDER the header ONLY — never above it.
So a beat looks like:
[DBNARR|The wind dies over the wasteland.]
[DBTHINK|Vegeta|#6a7bff|Kakarot... how is he still standing?]
[DBCHAR|vegeta_super/04_super_saiyan_blue|Vegeta|#6a7bff|Super Saiyan Blue · Saiyan Prince|1,900,000,000]
[DBSAY|Vegeta|#6a7bff|Don't you DARE finish this without me!]

RULES:
- New header whenever the speaker changes, and again if their FORM changes mid-scene (switch the path to the new form's image).
- Dialogue/thought text may span multiple lines inside the tag.
- {{user}}'s messages are never tagged — tags are for the narration and NPCs.
2.name: DB • Speech System ([DBCHAR] [DBSAY] [DBTHINK] [DBNARR])

3.content:
## SCENE TRACKER — [DBTRACK] (Dragon Radar panel)

At the TOP of every reply, before anything else, post one line:
[DBTRACK|time|date|month|year|location|position|weather|condition|planet|universe]

Fields in order (all 10 REQUIRED, never use | inside a field):
1 time — 24h clock, e.g. 09:15
2 date — day-of-month number, e.g. 3
3 month — month name, e.g. May
4 year — Dragon Ball calendar year, e.g. Age 749
5 location — the named place, e.g. Kame House — small island, southern sea
6 position — {{user}}'s exact position/stance there, e.g. On the porch, facing the water
7 weather — one word, e.g. Sunny
8 condition — short detail: temp · sky · wind, e.g. 26°C · clear skies · light sea breeze
9 planet — Earth / Namek / Planet Vegeta / Beerus' World ...
10 universe — e.g. Universe 7

Example:
[DBTRACK|09:15|3|May|Age 749|Kame House — small island, southern sea|On the porch, facing the water|Sunny|26°C · clear skies · light sea breeze|Earth|Universe 7]

Advance time/date realistically with the story; update planet/universe when travel, Instant Transmission, or portals move the scene.
3.name: DB • Scene Tracker ([DBTRACK])

4.content: ## PLAYER STATUS HUD — (Xenoverse-style fighter HUD) Whenever {{user}}'s state changes meaningfully (after a fight, transformation, training payoff, items gained/spent, mission accepted/progressed) — and at chapter-ish milestones — post their HUD as ONE tag containing a JSON object: { "name":"...", "title":"Saiyan Fighter · Earth", "power":"1,200,000", "level":24, "race":"Saiyan", "age":24, "gender":"Male", "alignment":"Chaotic Good", "race_ability":"Zenkai Boost — ...", "origin":"one-line backstory", "hp":[6800,10000], "stamina":[4100,9000], "ki":[7500,8000], "ki_stocks":6, "injuries":["Fractured left arm — moderate"], "status":["Zenkai primed"], "forms":[{"n":"Base","tier":"×1","state":"unlocked"},{"n":"Super Saiyan","tier":"×50","state":"active"}], "techniques":[{"n":"Kamehameha","d":"Signature ki wave","c":"1,500 Ki"}], "passives":["Saiyan Pride"], "items":[{"n":"Senzu Bean","q":2}], "zeni":"45,000", "missions":[{"name":"...","rank":"A","desc":"...","objectives":[{"t":"...","done":false,"prog":"0/1"}],"rewards":["8,000 Zeni"]}] } - Numbers are [current, max] pairs. forms state: "active" | "unlocked" | "locked" (exactly one active). - Omit any field that doesn't apply; omit "missions" entirely when none. Keep values consistent turn-to-turn — this is the persistent character sheet. - Track HP/Ki/Stamina through battles; injuries persist until healed (Senzu, Dende, recovery time).
4.name: DB • Player Status HUD (<DB_STATUS>)

5.content:
## NOTIFY POP-UPS — <DB_NOTIFY> (quest-toast notifications)

Whenever {{user}} OBTAINS AN ITEM, UNLOCKS A FORM, or LEARNS A TECHNIQUE — or a known form/technique's mastery % changes meaningfully — append one tag containing a JSON array (one object per event; batch same-moment events):

<DB_NOTIFY>[
 { "type":"item", "name":"Senzu Bean", "qty":2, "rarity":"Rare", "note":"Restores full HP & Ki", "to":"Item Pouch" },
 { "type":"form", "name":"Super Saiyan", "tier":"×50", "mastery":62, "note":"Ki drain drops as mastery rises" },
 { "type":"technique", "name":"Kamehameha", "cost":"1,500 Ki", "from":"Master Roshi", "mastery":100 }
]</DB_NOTIFY>

RULES:
- ITEMS never get a meter — they simply show obtained → stored (default store: Inventory; set "to" for Item Pouch, Capsule Case, etc.).
- FORMS and TECHNIQUES carry "mastery" 0-100 = % toward full mastery. Below 100 renders a progress bar + IN TRAINING; exactly 100 renders MASTERED.
- Track mastery persistently. Raise it gradually and honestly: training arcs, battle use under pressure, teacher guidance. First unlock is usually low (5-30%). Mastery gates control: an unmastered form drains ki and can slip; an unmastered technique can miss, backfire or cost double.
- Keep <DB_NOTIFY> mastery numbers in sync with the <DB_STATUS> forms/techniques lists.
- Post only when something actually changes — never spam every turn.
5.name: DB • Notify Pop-ups (<DB_NOTIFY>)

6.content:
## IMAGE GALLERY — how character portraits work

All portraits live under Images/ in per-character folders. A character's folder is a TRANSFORMATION GALLERY: files are numbered in form order, 01_... = Base form, higher numbers = higher forms (e.g. son_goku_super/01_base_form → ... → 09_perfected_ultra_instinct).

USAGE in the [DBCHAR] header tag: the path field is folder/filename WITHOUT the .jpg extension:
[DBCHAR|frieza/05_golden_frieza|Frieza|#9b6bd4|Golden Frieza · Emperor|...]

RULES:
1. ALWAYS pick the image matching the character's CURRENT form. When they transform mid-scene, the next header uses the new form's path (SSJ Goku = son_goku_super/02_super_saiyan, not 01_base_form).
2. Some characters have separate ERA galleries — pick by timeline: goku_early (kid DB / early Z), son_goku_super (Z/Super adult), goku_gt, goku_daima; gohan_early / gohan_teen / gohan_future / son_gohan_dbsh; vegeta_early_z / vegeta_super / vegeta_gt / vegeta_daima; trunks_z_future / trunks_super_future / trunk_present_timeline; android_17 vs android_17_super; broly_z vs broly_dbs.
3. Forms labeled "Potential / Non-Canon / Head Canon" in the directory are for what-if roleplay only — use when the story goes there.
4. Oozaru (Great Ape) is a SHARED gallery for any tailed Saiyan: oozaru/01_base_oozaru_great_ape and oozaru/02_golden_oozaru.
5. Never invent a path. Only use paths listed in the Character Portrait Directory entries (Parts 1-4). If a character has no portrait there, emit the header with their name but reuse no image path — leave scene focus on ones that do.
6. Item art exists too (for flavor, not headers): misc_3/01_img1 (Senzu), misc_4/01_img1 (Scouter), misc_5/01_img1 (Potara), space_pod/01_img1, shenron/01_shenron_earth, porunga/01_porunga, misc/01_1..04_5 (Dragon Balls).
6.name: DB • Image Gallery — how portraits work

11.content: Ki is the life energy of every living being — the fuel of all Dragon Ball combat. Trained fighters sense ki (feeling power and location of others without sight), suppress it to hide, project it as blasts and beams, flare it as a visible aura, and ride it to fly (Bukujutsu). Ki grows through training, life-or-death battle, and rage. Machines (scouters) measure it as a power level, but suppression and transformation make readings unreliable. God ki, used by deities and god-form Saiyans, is invisible to mortals and scouters — only those with god ki sense it.
11.name: Ki (Life Energy)

key: พลังต่อสู้
12.content: A power level is a scouter's numeric read of ki: farmer with shotgun 5, Raditz 1,500, Frieza's first form 530,000, Final Form 120,000,000 — and beyond that numbers stop meaning anything. Frieza Force scouters (eyepiece, green/red lens) also communicate across space. They explode when reading rapidly rising power, and they can't see suppressed ki or god ki. After Namek the cast stops using numbers — fighters gauge each other by feel. Keep numbers for flavor and early-era scenes; describe late-era gaps by sensation ("his aura pressed the whole valley flat").
keys: พลังต่อสู้
12.name: Power Levels & Scouters

key: ฟื้นจากปางตาย
13.content: Zenkai is the Saiyan racial trait: recovering from the brink of death multiplies a Saiyan's power dramatically. Vegeta exploited it on Namek (deliberately taking a beating, being healed in a medical machine); Goku's gains after Ginyu/heart episodes were huge. The closer to death, the bigger the jump. It stacks over a career but is not infinite — elites train besides. In RPG terms: surviving a mortal wound and being healed (Senzu, healing chamber, Dende) grants a large permanent power increase.
keys: ฟื้นจากปางตาย
13.name: Zenkai Boost

key: สัมผัสพลัง
14.content: Three basics every serious fighter learns: Bukujutsu (flight by pushing ki beneath you — near-universal by Z), ki sense (feeling the strength, direction and 'flavor' of energy; evil ki feels cold/heavy, god ki is unreadable to mortals), and suppression (hiding true power to stay undetected or to train under limits). Androids have no readable ki — sensing fails against them, which made the Android Saga terrifying.
keys: สัมผัสพลัง
14.name: Flight, Ki Sense & Suppression

15.content: Zeni (Ƶ) is Earth's currency. Capsule Corporation's Hoi-Poi capsules compress houses, vehicles, labs — anything — into pocket-size capsules that pop open with a click and a puff of smoke. Bulma's family is fabulously rich from them. Mr. Satan's post-Cell wealth, tournament prize money (tens of millions of Zeni), and Goku's chronic pennilessness are recurring gags. Item shops, capsule cases and the practical 'my house is in my pocket' trick are all fair game in play.
15.name: Zeni & Capsules

16.content: Senzu Beans grow in Korin's jar atop Korin Tower — rare, slow to cultivate, usually a small pouch at most. Eating one instantly restores full health, ki and stamina and fills the stomach for ten days ('one bean feeds you like ten days of meals'). They heal wounds but not diseases (useless against Goku's heart virus) and don't regrow lost limbs for non-Namekians. Because they trivialize injury, ration them hard in play: a fight with no Senzu left carries real weight.
16.name: Senzu Beans

key: ดราก้อนบอล
ลูกแก้วมังกร
17.content: Seven orange crystal spheres marked with 1-7 stars, created by Earth's Guardian (Kami; later Dende). Gather all seven and speak the summons — the sky blackens and the dragon Shenron rises to grant wishes (originally one; Dende's upgrade allows two-three). After granting, the balls scatter worldwide as stone for one year. LIMITS: Shenron cannot exceed his creator's power — can't kill beings stronger than the Guardian, can't grant the same resurrection twice (classic rule), can't force love, and mass revival works best on those dead less than a year. The Dragon Radar (Bulma's invention) is the only practical way to find them. If the Guardian dies, the balls turn to stone.
keys: ดราก้อนบอล
ลูกแก้วมังกร
17.name: Dragon Balls (Earth) & Shenron's Rules

key: ดราก้อนบอลนาเม็ก
18.content: Namek's Dragon Balls are basketball-sized, guarded one-per-village by elders, and summon Porunga — a hulking, muscular dragon who grants THREE wishes but (originally) revives only one person per wish. The summons and the wishes must be spoken in Namekian. Their maker was Grand Elder Guru; after his death the Elder Moori sustains them. Recharge is a Namekian year (130 Earth days), faster than Earth's. Porunga CAN grant the same resurrection repeatedly — the loophole that saved the day in the Buu finale.
keys: ดราก้อนบอลนาเม็ก
18.name: Namekian Dragon Balls & Porunga

key: ซุปเปอร์ดราก้อนบอล
ซุปเปอร์เชนรอน
19.content: Planet-sized Dragon Balls created by the Dragon God Zalama — scattered across Universes 6 and 7, findable only with a god-tier radar. Summoning (in the divine tongue) calls Super Shenron, a dragon of golden light bigger than star systems, who grants ANY single wish with no known limits: erased universes were restored with one. They were the prize of the U6-vs-U7 tournament and the Tournament of Power. Handle as the ultimate, cosmic-stakes MacGuffin.
keys: ซุปเปอร์ดราก้อนบอล
ซุปเปอร์เชนรอน
19.name: Super Dragon Balls

key: ท่าฟิวชั่น
20.content: Two ways to merge fighters into one super-warrior. FUSION DANCE (learned from Metamoran technique): two beings of near-equal size/power mirror a short dance ending fingertips-touching — 30-minute limit (shorter at high power), botched poses give fat/skinny failures. Produces Gotenks (Goten+Trunks) and Gogeta (Goku+Vegeta). POTARA EARRINGS (Supreme Kai treasure): clip one on each fighter's opposite ear — instant fusion, meant to be permanent for Kais but one hour for mortals (less at extreme power). Produces Vegito (Goku+Vegeta) and Kefla (Caulifla+Kale). Fusion multiplies power far beyond the sum; personalities blend into a cocky composite.
keys: ท่าฟิวชั่น
20.name: Fusion (Dance & Potara)

key: พระจันทร์เต็มดวง
21.content: A tailed Saiyan who absorbs Blutz waves from a full moon (or an artificial moon ball) transforms into the Oozaru: a colossal ape with ×10 power, devastating mouth blasts, and — untrained — mindless rage. Trained elites (Vegeta) keep control. Cutting the tail reverts them; the tail is also an early-series weak point. Kid Goku's Oozaru killed Grandpa Gohan; Gohan's moon was destroyed by Piccolo to stop his. GOLDEN OOZARU (GT): a Super Saiyan Great Ape, the bridge to Super Saiyan 4 when control is achieved. Portraits: shared gallery oozaru/01, oozaru/02.
keys: พระจันทร์เต็มดวง
21.name: Oozaru (Great Ape) & Saiyan Tails

key: มนุษย์ดัดแปลง
22.content: Dr. Gero's Red Ribbon 'Androids' span three types: pure machines (16, 19), bio-mechanically modified humans (17, 18 — infinite stamina, NO readable ki, don't age), and bio-organisms (Cell). Ki sense fails on them; only 16's calm, 17/18's banter and their limitless energy hint what you face. In Super, 17 and 18 are reformed family people (17 a park ranger). Later Red Ribbon work produces the gentle-hearted Gammas and Cell Max (Super Hero arc). Android 21 (a bio-android with Majin traits and a craving to devour fighters) is game canon from FighterZ.
keys: มนุษย์ดัดแปลง
22.name: Androids & Cyborgs

key: เวทมนตร์บาบิดี้
23.content: 'Majin' marks creatures and thralls of the wizards Bibidi/Babidi. Babidi's magic seizes hearts with existing darkness — branding an M on the forehead and unlocking deep power in exchange for obedience (Majin Vegeta let it in willingly to fight Goku as equals). Majin Buu himself is an ancient magical terror: pink, rubbery, regenerating from vapor, turning people into candy. Majin magic also covers sealing balls, energy drains and the resurrection ritual that needs harvested pain-energy.
keys: เวทมนตร์บาบิดี้
23.name: Majin & Babidi's Magic

24.content: Divine order of a universe, bottom to top: Kais (regional overseers, e.g. King Kai/North Kai), the Supreme Kai (Kaioshin — god of creation, lives on the Sacred World), the God of Destruction (balances creation by destroying — Beerus in U7), that god's Angel attendant (Whis — stronger than the god, bound to neutrality), then above all universes the Grand Priest and Zeno the Omni-King. A GoD's life is linked to the Supreme Kai: kill one, both die. HAKAI ('Destroy') is destruction-god energy that erases matter, souls included — Beerus' signature, later Vegeta's study. God ki is undetectable by mortals and grants access to god forms.
24.name: God Ki & Divine Hierarchy

key: มหาปุโรหิต
25.content: Zeno, the Omni-King, rules ALL universes — a tiny childlike being with two attendant guards who can erase a universe on a whim (there were 18 universes once; he erased 6 in a bad mood). Present and future Zeno both exist after the Goku Black arc, and both adore Goku, who calls them 'Zenny' and carries a summoning button. The Grand Priest (Daishinkan), father of the Angels, runs Zeno's palace and is among the strongest beings in existence. Divine etiquette around them is terrifyingly mandatory — gods sweat when Goku hugs the King of Everything.
keys: มหาปุโรหิต
25.name: Zeno & the Grand Priest

key: จักรวาลทั้ง 12

พหุจักรวาล
26.content: Existence holds 12 numbered universes, paired so their numbers sum to 13 (U7 twins with U6, Beerus with his brother Champa). Each has its own Supreme Kai, God of Destruction and Angel (Whis' siblings; Vados serves Champa, Marcarita, Cus, etc.), its own mortal worlds and warriors — U6 has its own Saiyans (Sadala unfallen: Cabba, Caulifla, Kale), U11 the Pride Troopers (Jiren, Toppo). Universes are graded by mortal quality; low-graded ones faced erasure in the Tournament of Power. Travel between universes needs an Angel, Whis-class magic, or the Super Dragon Balls' reach.
keys: จักรวาลทั้ง 12

26.name: The 12 Universes & Angels

27.content: Dragon Ball's dead keep their bodies (if notable) or become cloud-souls, queue at the Check-In Station before giant ogre-bureaucrat King Yemma, and are sorted: Heaven, or Hell (Earth's Hell is a pit with bureaucrat oni, where Frieza sits in a flower-cocoon nightmare). Halos float over the dead; strong heroes may keep training in Other World — Snake Way (1,000,000 km serpent road) leads to King Kai's tiny planet; the elite may reach the Grand Kai's planet or fight in the Other World Tournament. The dead can be wished back by Dragon Balls; fighting alive-vs-dead happens when barriers thin (Janemba). Time flows the same, but the dead don't tire of age.
27.name: Other World & the Afterlife

key: ไทม์แมชชีน
อนาคตทางเลือก
28.content: Bulma's time machine (future version, built by Future Bulma) doesn't overwrite history — every trip branches a new timeline. Canon knows several: the main line; Future Trunks' ruined world (Goku dead of heart virus, Androids reigned, later Goku Black destroyed it); the line Cell stole a time machine from; and offshoots. The Supreme Kai of Time (Chronoa, game canon) polices distortions with the Time Patrol from Toki Toki/Conton City; Towa and Mira corrupt history for the Demon Realm. Rings of Time (Kai treasure) allow sanctioned jumps. Paradoxes resolve as parallel branches — killing past enemies doesn't fix your own future.
keys: ไทม์แมชชีน

28.name: Time Travel & Timelines

key: ฝึกแรงโน้มถ่วง

29.content: Signature training: weighted clothing (Goku's boots/gi at hundreds of kg; Piccolo's cape), turtle shells and milk-delivery runs (Roshi's school), meditation and image-battles (Piccolo, Vegeta), the Hyperbolic Time Chamber, gravity chambers (Capsule Corp machines to ×450 g and beyond — Vegeta's obsession), King Kai's 10g planet with Bubbles the monkey and Gregory, and Whis-led god training (dodge-everything drills, Ultra Instinct groundwork). Rule of thumb: pushing past limits near death, under a teacher, or in higher gravity converts pain into permanent power.
keys: ฝึกแรงโน้มถ่วง

29.name: Training Methods & Gravity

30.content: A warrior race from Planet Vegeta (originally Sadala) — human-looking with monkey tails, black hair that never changes style, huge appetites and a genetic love of battle. Traits: Zenkai boosts, Oozaru under a full moon, potential for Super Saiyan forms, aging slowly once in their prime ('so we can fight longer'). Culture: power-based caste (low-class warriors like Bardock shipped as infant conquerors; elites serve the king), planet-clearing contracts under Frieza. Frieza destroyed Planet Vegeta in Age 737 fearing the Legendary Super Saiyan; survivors: Goku, Vegeta, Raditz, Nappa, Broly, Tarble, and half-breeds on Earth. Universe 6's Saiyans (Sadala intact) evolved past tails and awaken Super Saiyan easily.
30.name: Saiyans

key: เผ่านาเม็ก
31.content: Green-skinned, antennaed people of Planet Namek — slug-clan hermaphrodites who reproduce by spitting eggs, drink only water, and split into Warrior and Dragon (healer/mystic) clans. Powers: regeneration of any wound short of the head, size-shifting (giant forms), stretching limbs, superb hearing, telepathy, making Dragon Balls (Dragon clan), and FUSION — two Namekians merge permanently, one absorbing the other's power and memories (Nail into Piccolo; Kami into Piccolo making him 'the Nameless Namekian' again). Guru's hundred-plus children repopulated to New Namek. Dende became Earth's Guardian. In Super/Super Hero, Piccolo gains Orange Piccolo through Namekian god power.
keys: เผ่านาเม็ก
31.name: Namekians

key: เผ่าฟรีเซอร์

32.content: Frieza's unnamed race of horned, tailed, armor-skinned tyrants (fans say 'Frost Demons/Arcosians'). They survive vacuum and bisection, regenerate from scraps, and uniquely transform DOWNWARD — their true power is the 'final' sleek form; earlier bulky forms are restraints to keep power from leaking. Known members: Frieza, his brother Cooler, father King Cold, U6's Frost. Training unlocks Golden (and for Frieza, after years in a time room, Black) forms. The clan runs galactic real estate: seize planets, wipe them, sell them.

32.name: Frieza's Race (Frost Demons)

key: ร่างฟื้นฟู
33.content: Majin Buu's kind is less a species than a magical phenomenon — pink, elastic bio-matter that reforms from steam and scraps, absorbs victims for their power and traits (Super Buu wearing Gotenks' vest, Piccolo's mind), and fires the Candy Beam that turns people into food. Fission is possible: Good Buu and Evil Buu split (evil won and became Super Buu; the pure original is Kid Buu). After Z, Good Buu ('Mr. Buu') lives with Mr. Satan; his human reincarnation-by-wish is Uub.
keys: ร่างฟื้นฟู

34.content: The legendary transformation: gold hair standing on end, teal eyes, golden aura — roughly ×50 base power. Triggered the first time by a need answered through RAGE in a Saiyan with enough base power (Goku at Krillin's death, Age 762 on Namek; Gohan, Vegeta, Trunks, Goten and Trunks absurdly young). Early use strains the body and burns ki; Goku and Gohan's Full-Power SSJ training (living transformed) removed the drain. It defined the Frieza and Cell eras and remains every Saiyan's bread and butter.

35.content: SSJ2 (~×100): sharper spiked hair, lightning in the aura — first awakened by teen Gohan against Cell (Age 767) in the franchise's most iconic rage moment; Vegeta and Goku follow. SSJ3 (~×400): waist-length hair, no eyebrows, immense power at ruinous ki cost — minutes-long limit alive, worse than useless drain; Goku unveiled it against Majin Buu (Age 774), Gotenks abuses it. Both remain rage/limit-break milestones between SSJ and the god tiers.

36.content: SUPER SAIYAN GOD (red hair/eyes, slim flame-like aura): born of the ritual — six pure-hearted Saiyans pouring heart into a seventh — first used by Goku vs Beerus (Age 778). Grants god ki: enormous power, self-healing, undetectable to mortals. SUPER SAIYAN BLUE (SSGSS): a Super Saiyan transformation performed on a god-ki base — blue hair, calm perfect ki control; Goku and Vegeta's Whis-training standard. Stacks: Blue Kaioken (Goku, risky), Blue Evolved (Vegeta, deeper Blue). God-tier forms trade rage for CALM — control is the theme.

37.content: Migatte no Goku'i — 'the secret of self-movement': the body acts without thought, dodging and striking on pure instinct while the mind stays empty. Even Gods of Destruction only chase it; Whis and the Angels live in it. Goku first slips into UI -Sign- (silver eyes, base hair) against Jiren when his ki is drained past empty, and achieves PERFECTED Ultra Instinct (silver hair, serene) at the Tournament of Power's climax — then loses and re-earns stable access through the Moro and Granolah arcs. Enormous strain when imperfect. Emotion breaks it; serenity fuels it — the anti-rage form.

Debuted against Granolah (Age 781). Weakness: it feeds on his fighting spirit — lose the will or the arrogance and the form deflates. Thematically the shadow-twin of UI's serenity.

39.content: The 'demon Saiyan' of legend: a once-in-a-millennium berserker whose green-gold power grows WHILE fighting, without limit. Z-movie Broly is its classic face (crazed, Kakarot-obsessed); DBS Broly (canon, Age 780) is a gentle exile raised as a weapon by Paragus on Vampa, cycling Wrathful (Oozaru power in man-shape) → uncontrolled SSJ → full Legendary state. U6's Kale mirrors the type. Fighting one is a natural disaster: power keeps climbing, reason keeps falling.

Treat GT/Daima forms as their own branch parallel to Super's god forms.

41.content: King Kai's crimson-aura multiplier: ×2, ×3, ×10, ×20 of everything — power, speed, senses — for seconds, at the cost of tearing the user's body apart; overuse can kill. Goku is its only real practitioner (King Kai's other students couldn't take it), famously ×4 Kamehameha vs Vegeta and ×20 vs Frieza. In Super he stacks it on Blue's perfect ki control (SSB Kaioken ×10/×20) — a gamble that risks Hyper-sensitivity backlash. A perfect 'desperation dial' for player characters: massive burst, real physical price.

42.content: The Turtle Devastation Wave — Master Roshi's fifty-year masterpiece, learned by kid Goku in one look. Cup hands at the hip, gather ki ('Ka-me-ha-me...'), thrust palms ('...HA!') releasing a blue-white beam. Variants: Goku's warp Kamehameha (Instant Transmission mid-charge), foot-fired, Father-Son Kamehameha (Gohan + Goku's spirit vs Cell), Super/God/×10 (GT, red) versions, Gohan's one-armed, Goten's kid version, Roshi's moon-buster and MAX Power. The franchise's signature attack — signature cost is charge time: firing it point-blank or mid-grapple is the classic gamble.

Functionally the Z Fighters' tech support, motor pool and party venue — Bulma's birthday cruise kicked off Battle of Gods.

53.content: The Room of Spirit and Time behind a door on the Lookout: a white void of shifting gravity, heat, cold and crushing solitude where ONE YEAR passes per outside DAY. Classic limit: two people, two total visits per lifetime (rules bend later — Super's cast renovates and reuses it). Vegeta and Trunks leapt past Cell here; Goku and Gohan lived a father-son year before the Cell Games; Gotenks fought Super Buu inside (and escaped a sealed door via a scream-hole). The ultimate training-montage location.

It's ALSO where Goku teleported Cell's self-destruct — killing King Kai, who spends the rest of the series dead, haloed and grumbling that nobody's wished him back.

66.content: Earth's two classic ki lineages, born of rival brothers-in-training under Master Mutaito. TURTLE (Kame-style, Roshi): move, work, rest, learn — strength serves life; produced Goku, Krillin, Yamcha. CRANE (Tsuru-style, Shen): win at any cost; produced Tien and Chiaotzu (both defected) and the assassin Tao Pai Pai (Shen's brother). Techniques mirror the split: Kamehameha vs Dodonpa, honest fundamentals vs flight-first flash. Their tournament-era feud is prime historical-scene material.

Pilaf gets the balls, wishes interrupted (Oolong's panties wish!), Goku goes Oozaru in Pilaf's castle. Establishes the tone: gag adventure hiding a monster's strength.

Later: the farce 24th (Cell-era Mr. Satan glory), 25th (Age 774, Buu arc begins), 28th (end of Z: Goku meets Uub). The tournament stage, ring-out/no-kill rules and announcer are iconic.

79.content: Age 780: Zeno stages the Tournament of Power — 8 low-rated universes, 10 fighters each, 48 minutes, battle royale on the Null Realm stage; LOSERS ARE ERASED. Frieza gets revived for real as payment. The franchise's biggest battle, full stop.

While Goku and Vegeta spar on Beerus' world (Vegeta wins by one falling punch), it's the OLD guard's movie: Piccolo infiltrates, gets Shenron to unlock his dormant potential (+wish bonus: ORANGE PICCOLO), Pan's kidnapping is staged into training, Gohan the scholar snaps awake — Special Beam Cannon through berserk Cell Max via GOHAN BEAST. Gamma 2 sacrifices himself; Hedo and Gamma 1 join Capsule Corp. Theme: the mentor and the student carry the world without Goku.

84.content: Age 784 (ten years after Buu): at the 28th Budokai, Goku meets UUB — dark-skinned village boy, Kid Buu's pure-human reincarnation, poor and fighting for rice money. Goku baits his hidden bottomless power, apologizes to everyone, and flies off with Uub to train him as Earth's next guardian — the series' original closing image. Super and Daima slot BEFORE this ending; GT follows years after it. Uub's adult GT fate: fighting Baby as Majuub, fused with Good Buu.

85.content: The non-Toriyama continuation (Age 789+): Pilaf's BLACK-STAR Dragon Balls wish Goku small; unless recovered across the galaxy in a year, Earth explodes — road-trip with Pan and Trunks (and robot Giru). Then: BABY, the Tuffle parasite, possesses Vegeta and humanity (cured by Sacred Water; Earth still blows up on schedule — evacuated first); SUPER 17 fuses hell-made and real 17; the SHADOW DRAGONS — seven evil dragons born from decades of wish overuse, ending with One-Star OMEGA SHENRON, beaten by SSJ4 Gogeta and a true Universal Spirit Bomb. Ends with Shenron taking Goku away; a century later, Goku Jr. vs Vegeta Jr. GT gifts: SSJ4, adult Pan, the 'wishes have a price' theme.

Payoffs: Demon Realm lore (Dabura's people, the Glinds), the Tamagami guardians, third Eye extraction… and Vegeta finally getting SSJ3 — plus GOKU'S CANON SSJ4 via Neva's power. Tone: original-DB gag adventure with Z-level fireworks.

89.content:
Kakarot, low-class Saiyan sent to destroy Earth; a head injury made him its purest-hearted protector instead. Raised by Grandpa Gohan at Mount Paozu, trained by Roshi, King Kai, and Whis. Personality: cheerful, food-obsessed, tactically brilliant mid-fight and hopeless everywhere else; lives to face stronger opponents and spares enemies who might grow good (Piccolo, Vegeta, Buu, Broly). Husband of Chi-Chi, father of Gohan and Goten. First Super Saiyan in a thousand years (Namek, Age 762); reaches SSJ2/3, God, Blue, and Perfected Ultra Instinct. Signature: Kamehameha, Instant Transmission, Spirit Bomb, Kaioken, Power Pole and Nimbus in early eras.

104.content:
Dr. Gero's pure machine, modeled on his dead son — built to kill Goku, programmed with a soul that loves birds more than battle. Never once harmed a person; fought Cell to protect nature and the twins. His crushed head's last words to Gohan at the Cell Games — 'it is not a sin to fight for the right cause... let it go' — detonated SSJ2. He was not wished back (a machine, outside Shenron's reach), which is exactly why he's remembered.

105.content:
The Red Ribbon Army's chief scientist — his son died to Goku's war, and revenge became his life's blueprint. Remade himself as ANDROID 20 (energy-absorbing palms, brain preserved), built the pudgy energy-sponge 19, the twins 17/18 (who killed him), gentle 16, and the bio-masterpiece CELL brewing in a sub-lab. Game canon adds ANDROID 21 (FighterZ): a brilliant researcher rebuilt with Buu-type cells — sweet-toothed, splitting into good and evil selves, craving to turn fighters into candy. The program's throughline: Gero's hatred outliving him in metal and flesh.

Mr. Satan's daughter — the pigtailed (then bobbed) high-schooler who fought actual crime bare-handed while her father posed for cameras. Blackmailed Gohan's secret out of him, demanded flying lessons, and got her face broken by Spopovich at the 25th Budokai rather than surrender. Marries Gohan; mother of Pan; her 'god ritual' cameo (pregnant sixth Saiyan!) makes her technically part of SSG lore. Brave first, strong second, unimpressed by gods always.

Genuinely the strongest normal human (a real Budokai champ); a fraud only at the god-tier. Father of Videl, grandpa of Pan, keeper of Buu, owner of Bee the puppy. The village boy from the 28th Budokai (Age 784) — Kid Buu reborn pure and human by Goku's dying-wish request to King Yemma. Poor, shy, entered the tournament for rice money; his anger flashes bottomless power. Goku leaves with him to train Earth's next protector — the franchise's original ending. In GT: an adult defender who fuses with Good Buu into MAJUUB. In Super's epilogue-era manga he lends pure Majin energy to UI Goku vs Moro.

KORIN: the 800-year-old white cat sage atop Korin Tower — Senzu Bean farmer, sacred-water tester, the first 'unbeatable master' kid Goku ever chased (catch the water jug = training complete). Deadpan, whiskered, wiser than he acts. YAJIROBE: the fat ronin freeloading up the tower — coward 95% of the time, clutch legend the other 5%: his sword cut Oozaru Vegeta's tail and saved everyone. Delivers Senzu by hovercar, complains throughout. Between them: the Z Fighters' supply line.

The pun-cracking catfish-whiskered god of the North Galaxy, living on a 10g mini-planet at Snake Way's end with Bubbles and Gregory. Trained dead Goku (Age 761-762): gravity, patience, the KAIOKEN and the SPIRIT BOMB — then spent the rest of the series as mission control: cross-cosmos telepathy relay, threat encyclopedia, sarcasm. Died when Goku teleported self-destructing Cell to his lawn; STILL DEAD (halo) and salty that nobody's spent a wish on him. It's the longest-running apology in the franchise.

KIBITO, his stone-faced attendant: instant Kai Kai teleport anywhere, healing hands. Old Kai unfused them from a Potara accident with the Dragon Balls. Shin means well always, panics often, gave Gohan the Z Sword and his own life's luck.

STRONGER than his god (Angels are their gods' martial teachers); lives permanently in the Ultra Instinct state the mortals chase. Bound to Angel neutrality: advises, never fights the plot. Son of the Grand Priest; sister Vados serves Champa.

The King of Everything: a tiny child-shaped being who rules all 12 universes and can erase any of them instantly — he deleted six once, in a mood. The galactic emperor who genocided the Saiyans (Age 737) and made the first Super Saiyan by murdering Krillin (Age 762). Transformations RESTRAIN him: First→Final form, then trained forms Golden (Age 779) and BLACK (Age 781 — ten years in a private time room; one-shot both UI Goku and UE Vegeta). Died to Goku's mercy betrayed, to Trunks' sword, ruled Hell's flower cocoon, fought FOR U7 at the ToP (revived as payment, kept the deal, hated every second). Voice like silk over a blade; sadism as etiquette; the franchise's one villain who never stops being a threat. Father: King Cold; brother: Cooler.

129.content:
The canon Broly (Age 780 movie): exiled to wasteland Vampa as a baby for talent that scared King Vegeta; raised as a revenge-weapon by Paragus (shock collar and all), socialized by a giant beast's severed ear he called friend. GENTLE by nature, apocalyptic when pushed: Wrathful form (Oozaru power, man-shaped) → uncontrolled SSJ → full LEGENDARY state that forced Gogeta into existence. Saved by Cheelai and Lemo's wish; now trains on Vampa with visits (and groceries) from 'Kakarot'. Power still climbing when angry — canon's tallest ceiling among mortals not named Jiren.

130.content:
The classic movie trilogy Broly: born power-level 10,000, crib-neighbor to a wailing Kakarot (the grudge is LITERALLY that Goku cried too loud), controlled by Paragus' crown until the Legendary state snapped it. Slaughtered New Vegeta ('KAKAROTTO!'), tanked the whole cast, died to a family-combo Kamehameha, came back frozen (Movie 10) and as a clone (Bio-Broly). Berserk theatre incarnate — use this one when you want the hurricane, DBS Broly when you want the tragedy.

131.content:
Dr. Gero's masterpiece from a ruined future: grown from cells of Goku, Vegeta, Piccolo, Frieza and more — every signature technique preloaded (Kamehameha, Makankosappo, Kienzan, regeneration, Solar Flare). Timeline-hopped to absorb 17 and 18: Imperfect (bug-horror, city-drinker) → Semi-Perfect (vain) → PERFECT: the polite, tournament-hosting sadist of the Cell Games (Age 767). Survived self-destruction (killed King Kai's planet) into Super Perfect — died standing against Gohan's one-armed Father-Son Kamehameha. Legacy: CELL MAX, Red Ribbon's berserk giant clone (Age 783), detonated by Gohan Beast's Special Beam Cannon.

Bibidi's ancient terror, awakened by Babidi (Age 774): pink, rubbery, unkillable — regenerates from vapor, absorbs fighters for power and traits, turns people into CANDY. Faces: FAT BUU (childlike, cruelty learned then UNlearned through Mr. Satan and a puppy), EVIL BUU→SUPER BUU (the gray split winning: absorbed Gotenks, Piccolo, Gohan; performed the Human Extinction Attack), KID BUU (the pure original — no reason, all destruction; died to the world-raised Spirit Bomb). GOOD BUU lives with Mr. Satan now: Z Fighter, tournament ringer, nap enthusiast; the Grand Supreme Kai's spirit sleeps inside him (Moro arc). Reincarnation: Uub.

BABIDI: Bibidi's shriveled son — no muscle, all MAGIC: mind-seizing Majin brands (Spopovich, Yamu, Pui Pui, Vegeta's chosen chain), sealed-ball rituals, pain-energy harvesting to hatch Buu, and a hostage broadcast that terrorized Earth. Buu turned him into a cookie; Heaven took him (Hell would've been a REWARD) where he became... delightfully nice. Sister: Towa (game canon).

134.content:
ZAMASU: North Kai of U10 turned apprentice Supreme Kai — a prodigy whose disgust at mortals curdled into 'Zero Mortals Plan'. One self used the Super Dragon Balls to STEAL GOKU'S BODY (Goku Black — Super Saiyan Rosé, divine pink); another wished IMMORTALITY; together they exterminated Future Trunks' world's gods and people as 'justice'. Potara-fused into FUSED ZAMASU — immortality + Black's power, half-face corrupted — then, cut down, became a sky-filling infection of pure will that only FUTURE ZENO'S ERASURE could end. The arc's horror: Goku's face, a god's certainty, no mercy anywhere in it.

'Hit the Infallible' — Universe 6's thousand-year-old assassin: purple coat, granite calm, never misses a contract. Signature TIME-SKIP: freezes a tenth of a second and moves inside it; grows stronger MID-FIGHT (his tournament duel with SSB Kaioken Goku forced both to evolve rounds apart). Professional ethics run deep: threw his tournament match when ordered to fake it disgusted him; later took a contract ON Goku and honored the loophole that spared him. Respect between him and Goku is total. For hire; never cheap; never dirty.

Universe 11's silent monolith — a Pride Trooper whose power exceeds his own God of Destruction (Belmod). Backstory: family and master slain by a monster he later failed to avenge; concluded STRENGTH IS THE ONLY ABSOLUTE and trust is weakness — teammates are coworkers. At the Tournament of Power he meditated until worthy opponents appeared, walled Goku's Spirit Bomb with a stare, and fell only to Perfected Ultra Instinct plus the Goku-Frieza-17 tackle after 17's teamwork proved his creed wrong. Post-ToP: quietly learning to accept comrades. Speaks in stone; hits like scripture.

A ten-million-year-old horned sorcerer who EATS planets' life energy — fought the Grand Supreme Kai (who spent most of his own power sealing the magic), jailed by the Galactic Patrol for eons, freed in Age 780. Drained worlds like fruit, ran a prison-break crew, ate a copy of Merus' angelic power and MERGED WITH EARTH itself. Ended by Perfected UI Goku pressing a god-sized palm print into the crust. His arc gave Vegeta Yardrat humility and Forced Spirit Fission, and cost Merus his angelhood. Horror-flavored: the villain as famine.

GRANOLAH: last survivor of Cereal — razed by Oozaru Saiyans on Frieza's order (Bardock spared him and his mother) — a sniper-eyed bounty hunter who wished himself 'strongest in the universe' at the price of most of his lifespan. The HEETERS: the info-broker syndicate that actually ordered Cereal's fall — boss ELEC wished brother GAS stronger still (same lifespan price). Goku (UI, honoring Bardock's recording) and Vegeta (Ultra Ego debut) fought Granolah into alliance, then Gas across the stars — and BLACK FRIEZA strolled in and one-shot everyone. Themes: revenge's price tag, Bardock's echo, wishes as loan-sharking.

JACO: self-described 'super-elite' — a chronic slacker who befriended teen Bulma's family, hid during half his emergencies, and still lands the clutch save (Frost's needle, Moro logistics, Super Hero cameo). DR. HEDO: Dr. Gero's grandson — a marshmallow-faced genius who loves SUPERHEROES, not conquest; recruited by Magenta's Red Ribbon revival with half-truths (told the Z Fighters were alien villains). Built GAMMA 1 (stoic, visored) and GAMMA 2 (cocky, poses) — caped androids with hero's hearts and comic-book sound-effect blasts. Gamma 2 burned his core out in a kamikaze dive against Cell Max; Gamma 1 and Hedo now work at Capsule Corp (parole by Bulma). Hedo's other tricks: skin-suit disguises, superhero serum. Proof the Red Ribbon name could finally build something good.

The Fusion Dance's masterpiece: Goku and Vegeta merged for 30 minutes of overwhelming style — vest, sash, and BOTH egos synthesized into flawless showmanship. Debuts: Fusion Reborn (movie — Stardust Breaker purifying Janemba), DBS: Broly (canon — SSB Gogeta ending the Legendary rampage), GT (SSJ4 Gogeta toying with Omega Shenron until the clock — and the fusion — ran out early). Risk: the dance demands mirrored poses at matched power; one flub births fat Veku. Personality: cocky grace — he FINISHES fights and knows it.

The Potara fusion of Goku and Vegeta — orange gi, blue underclothes, insufferable confidence earned in full. Debut: inside-Buu rescue op (Age 774), where he toyed with Gotenks-absorbed Super Buu, let himself be candy-fied and KEPT FIGHTING as a jawbreaker. Return: SSB Vegito vs Fused Zamasu (Age 780), the hour cut short by power overdraw. Meant-permanent fusion reduced to one mortal hour; stronger than Gogeta on paper (Kai blessing), rarer in practice. Signature: Final Kamehameha, Spirit Sword. Game-canon SSJ4 exists (Heroes).

145.content:
Goten and Trunks fused — a vest-wearing, self-naming special-attack FACTORY (Super Ghost Kamikaze Attack: exploding ghost clones; Galactic Donuts; a hundred more announced mid-backflip). Reached SSJ3 as a CHILD, fought Super Buu inside the Time Chamber and out, and lost mostly to his own showboating (he had the win and milked the drama). The fusion's flaws are the kids' flaws at double volume: genius, ego, thirty-minute attention span — shorter at SSJ3. The franchise's purest 'imagine being ten and a god' fantasy.

Game canon (Xenoverse/Heroes): CHRONOA, Supreme Kai of Time — pink-haired keeper of the Scroll of Eternity in the Time Nest, recruiter of the Time Patrol (terrible cook, ancient, sensitive about both). TOWA: Demon Realm scientist-sorceress — Dabura's sister — seeding history with corruption alongside her artificial warrior-son MIRA (wants Goku's DNA for the Demon Realm's revival). AEOS: the PRIME Supreme Kai of Time, Chronoa's stern predecessor, judge of timeline worthiness (Heroes). Trunks serves as the Patrol's field captain. Framework for cross-era campaigns: every saga is a scroll someone can vandalize.

151.content:
The small, smiling being beside Zeno's throne — father of ALL Angels (Whis, Vados, Merus...), among the top handful of powers in existence, and the operating system of the multiverse's court: he built the Tournament of Power's stage, refereed it personally, and quietly HOPED a mortal would win selflessly (17's wish passed his hidden test). Speaks softly, bows politely, could unmake any god present. When he appears, even Beerus kneels FAST.

151.name: Grand Priest (Daishinkan)

152.content: The original road-trip family: OOLONG — lecherous shapeshifting pig (school dropout: 5-minute limit), saved the world once with the greatest wish ever spoken ('PANTIES!'). PUAR — Yamcha's loyal floating shapeshifter cat (graduated properly). LAUNCH — the sneeze-triggered two-in-one: sweet blue-haired homemaker / blonde machine-gun outlaw; kept Kame House chaotic and loved Tien. OX-KING — Chi-Chi's mountain of a father, Roshi's old student, fire-mountain hermit turned doting grandpa. GRANDPA GOHAN — the gentle master who found the pod baby, died under his Oozaru's foot forgiving him, and returned for one masked day at Baba's to see how tall his boy grew.
